---
description: "Use when writing product copy, marketing text, brand content, email templates, or any user-facing text for EcoReusable."
applyTo: "copy-bank/**"
---

# Eco-Friendly Copy Guidelines — EcoReusable

## Tone

- **Aspirational, not preachy** — inspire action through positive framing, not guilt
- **Warm and conversational** — write like a knowledgeable friend, not a textbook
- **Confident but humble** — state benefits clearly without overclaiming
- **Specific and honest** — use precise language; vague eco-claims erode trust

## Do

- Lead with the benefit to the buyer ("Keeps drinks cold for 24 hours")
- Frame sustainability as a lifestyle upgrade, not a sacrifice
- Use specific, verifiable eco-claims ("Made from FSC-certified bamboo")
- Include practical details (care instructions, dimensions, compatibility)
- Write scannable content (short paragraphs, bullet points, clear headings)

## Don't

- Use guilt or fear ("The planet is dying because of people like you")
- Make vague or unsubstantiated claims ("100% eco-friendly", "saves the planet")
- Use scientifically inaccurate terms ("chemical-free", "all-natural" without context)
- Lecture or moralize — the customer already cares, that's why they're here
- Sacrifice clarity for cleverness — clear beats creative every time

## Voice Reference

Always read `docs/tone-of-voice.md` before writing. If it doesn't exist yet, follow the principles above and flag that the tone document needs to be created.

## SEO Integration

- Naturally incorporate target keywords — never stuff
- Every product and page needs: SEO title (≤60 chars), meta description (≤155 chars)
- Use semantically rich headings (H1 for page title, H2 for sections)
